# {Project Name}

> {One- or two-sentence description of what this project is and does. Write it
> for an agent deciding whether this is the right tool and where to look next.}

{Optional: one short paragraph of essential context — the single most important
thing to know before using it.}

## Docs

- [Full agent manual](llms-full.txt): cheat sheet, rules, traps, recipes, debugging — read this first for code generation.
- [README](README.md): human-oriented overview and quick start.
- [API / reference]({link}): full reference for {types, endpoints, tags, ...}.
- [Specification / ground truth]({link}): authoritative behavior spec.

## Examples

- [{Example name}]({link}): {what it shows}.

## For contributors

- [AGENTS.txt](AGENTS.txt): invariants and the extension checklist for agents modifying this code.

<!--
This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llms.txt.org): an H1 title, a
blockquote summary, then H2 sections of markdown links. Keep it short — it is an
index, not the manual. The substance lives in llms-full.txt.
Place this file at the repository root (and, ideally, served at /llms.txt).
-->
